fields.description:
## Why
ADR 0047 relaxes pre-merge re-CI on a clean rebase, so the detector for a rare semantic conflict between two individually-green changes is the EXISTING post-merge `main` CI, with a MANUAL revert as the remedy (no auto-revert — deliberate cost/scope choice). ADR 0047 promised this runbook ("runbook to follow"); this task delivers it.

## What
- [ ] Create `infra/runbooks/main-red-post-merge.md`: document (a) the detector — `.github/workflows/test.yml` runs the full suite on every `push` to `main` (branches-ignore feature/**) after Gerrit replicates the submit to the GitHub mirror; where to watch it; that it is a post-merge *detector*, not a pre-merge gate; (b) the manual-revert procedure — confirm-not-flake, identify the culprit change, revert THROUGH Gerrit (revert change → two-vote gate → Submit), confirm green, re-land correctly; (c) that auto-revert is deliberately NOT built (revisit only if red-main recurs).
- [ ] Cross-link the runbook from ADR 0047 (replace its "runbook to follow" placeholder) and from CONTRIBUTING.md's post-merge safety-net note.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Runbook present + covers the three parts: `test -f infra/runbooks/main-red-post-merge.md` AND `grep -qi "test.yml" infra/runbooks/main-red-post-merge.md && grep -qi "manual revert" infra/runbooks/main-red-post-merge.md && grep -qi "auto-revert is deliberately NOT built" infra/runbooks/main-red-post-merge.md` all succeed.
- [ ] Cross-linked both ways: `grep -q "infra/runbooks/main-red-post-merge.md" docs/adr/0047-retire-autolander-rebase-if-necessary.md` AND `grep -q "infra/runbooks/main-red-post-merge.md" CONTRIBUTING.md` both succeed; ADR 0047 no longer contains the literal "runbook to follow".
- [ ] docs-index / dead-link checker green: `python scripts/check_docs_index.py --check` exits 0.
- [ ] make lint && make typecheck && make test green.
